behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real diarization/model output quality from the speech-to-text API, not just local wiring. A stubbed transcript with perfect labels would hide real speaker-label loss (MOCK-HIDE). I considered Workflow, but behavior text focuses on STT label preservation; it does not require validating multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real speech-to-text API and assert speaker labels are preserved and usable for attendee attribution downstream.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is local parsing/structuring logic and can fail within one function/object boundary (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor). No required real OS/network/subprocess or model judgment. I considered Integration, but behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff to manifest the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript text strings into the parser function and assert extracted assignee/task fields match expected structured input objects.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM summary content quality (coverage per assignee). Deterministic fixtures can verify routing but cannot reveal misses in model-generated action items (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). I considered Workflow, but this can be validated on a single summarization step from one transcript.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a real transcript with known assignee-task pairs to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in live summary output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text scopes to routing logic after decisions are already identified. That is deterministic mapping logic, testable without real infra or model output. I considered Agentic, but "identified decisions" explicitly disqualifies dependence on model judgment for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the decision-routing function with pre-identified decision items and assert output populates only the `decisions` schema field, not narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is model output placement/quality: the LLM must recognize decision content and place it correctly. Mocked outputs would trivially pass and hide real extraction/placement misses (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). I considered Workflow, but behavior can be checked in one live summarization call.
behaviors.test_description: Use a transcript containing clear decision statements with a real LLM summarization call and assert those decisions appear in the `decisions` section rather than only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing across segments plus merged coherence, which depends on multiple agentic calls and handoff/order across steps (MULTI-STEP). Integration with stubs can test splitting mechanics, but would miss real cross-segment coherence failures from model outputs. I considered Agentic, but a single model call is disqualified by the behavior's sequential segmented pipeline text.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation settings, execute real sequential transcription/summarization across segments, and assert merged output is a single coherent structured summary with no dropped segment content.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end agent pipeline with real upload, transcription, summarization, and delivery. It combines real infrastructure and multiple agentic stages, so lower levels miss cross-stage failures and operator-free completion risks. I considered System, but behavior text explicitly spans multiple agentic steps in sequence, which disqualifies System as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting audio file: upload, real STT, real LLM summarization, and output delivery; assert completion succeeds automatically with valid structured schema.
